content:
# Ground Motion Prediction

**Summary**: Equations used to predict ground motion parameters based on earthquake magnitude, distance, and site conditions.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-latif|Latif, 2015]] developed ground-motion-prediction equations for subduction-zone earthquakes in Java Island.

- [[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025]] developed Ground Motion Prediction Models (GMPMs) for the West Java region to enhance the Indonesia Earthquake Early Warning System.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-latif|Latif, 2015]] Essential for probabilistic seismic hazard analysis (PSHA) to estimate ground shaking.

- [[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025]] developed Ground Motion Prediction Models (GMPMs) for the West Java region to enhance the Indonesia Earthquake Early Warning System.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-latif|Latif, 2015]] Regression of recorded strong-motion amplitude data.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The resulting predictions are generally lower than those predicted by Youngs et al. (1997) and significantly higher for moderate events than those of Atkinson and Boore (2003). ([[2015-latif|Latif, 2015, p. 21]])


- The CatBoost model outperformed other models across all three tectonic categories with high Pearson correlation coefficients (0.86, 0.90, 0.91). ([[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025, p. 14]])
